tests.description: Should pass
Should fail
Should pass when const is set
Should pass when const is set without maxLength (const bounds value space)
Should fail when const type does not match declared type
Should pass when enum is set
Should pass when enum is set without maxLength (enum bounds value space)
Should fail when enum is not set
Should fail when enum is not set
Should fail when enum values do not match declared type
Should pass when type is [string,null] with enum and no maxLength (enum bounds value space)
Should pass when format is set to uuid
Should pass when format is set to date-time
Should pass when format is set to email
Should pass when format is set to uri
Should fail when format is not set
Should fail when format is set and maxLength is not set
Should fail when format is iso-date-time (vendor-specific, use date-time instead)
Should fail when format is byte (vendor-specific, not in JSON Schema spec)
Should fail when format is int32 (vendor-specific, not in JSON Schema spec)
Should fail when format is unknown
Should fail when format is url (non-standard, use uri instead)
Should fail when format is url-reference (non-standard, use uri-reference instead)
Should pass when pattern is set
Should fail when pattern is set and maxLength is not set
Should fail when pattern is not set
Should fail when pattern missing leading ^
Should fail when pattern missing trailing $
Should pass when pattern contains `\.` char
Should fail when pattern contains `.` char
Should pass when pattern contains `.` inside character class [a-z.]
Should pass when pattern contains `.` inside character class [.]
Should fail when pattern contains `.` outside character class after [...]
Should fail when pattern has overlapping quantifiers [a-z]+[a-z]+
Should fail when pattern has overlapping quantifiers [a-z]*[a-z]+
Should fail when pattern has overlapping quantifiers \d+\d+
Should pass when pattern has non-overlapping adjacent classes [a-z]+[0-9]+
Should pass when pattern has adjacent class without second quantifier [a-z]+[a-z]
Should fail when pattern has semantically overlapping quantifiers \d+[0-9]+
Should fail when pattern has semantically overlapping quantifiers [0-9]+\d+
Should fail when pattern has semantically overlapping quantifiers \d*[0-9]+
Should fail when pattern has semantically overlapping quantifiers [0-9]*\d+
Should pass when pattern has \d followed by [a-z] (non-overlapping)
Should fail when pattern has semantically overlapping quantifiers \w+\d+ (superset overlap)
Should fail when pattern has semantically overlapping quantifiers \d+\w+ (superset overlap)
Should fail when pattern has semantically overlapping quantifiers \w*\d+ (superset overlap)
Should pass when pattern has \w followed by \s (disjoint, no overlap)
Should fail when pattern contains `\S` char
Should fail when pattern contains `\W` char
Should fail when pattern contains `\D` char
Should fail when pattern contains `\v` (vertical tab, control char U+000B)
Should fail when pattern contains `\f` (form feed, control char U+000C)
Should fail when pattern contains `\C` char
Should fail when pattern contains nested quantifier `(a+)+`
Should fail when pattern contains nested quantifier `([a-z]+)*`
Should fail when pattern contains unbounded group repeat `(a+){2,}` (equivalent to (a+)+)
Should pass when pattern contains optional group `(?:abc)?`
Should pass when pattern contains exact group repeat `(?:abc){3}`
Should pass when pattern contains bounded group repeat `(?:abc){3,5}`
Should pass when pattern contains non-nested quantifier
Should pass when pattern contains unicode character class escape
Should fail when pattern contains empty negated character class [^]
Should fail when pattern contains empty negated character class [^] with +
Should fail when negated character class has content [^a] (broad denylist)
Should fail when pattern contains \P{L} (negated unicode property escape)
Should fail when pattern contains \P{N} in character class
Should fail when pattern contains positive lookahead (?=...)
Should fail when pattern contains positive lookbehind (?<=...)
Should pass when pattern contains non-capturing group (?:...)
Should fail when pattern contains negative lookahead (?!...)
Should fail when negative lookahead contains alternation
Should fail when negative lookbehind contains alternation
Should fail when pattern contains negative lookbehind (?<!...)
Should fail when pattern contains bare alternation (breaks anchoring)
Should fail when pattern has alternation with quantifiers (breaks anchoring)
Should pass when alternation is inside non-capturing group
Should pass when alternation is inside nested groups
Should pass when alternation is inside depth-3 right-nested groups
Should pass when alternation is inside depth-3 left-nested groups
Should pass when alternation is inside depth-4 nested groups
Should pass when alternation is inside depth-5 nested groups
Should fail when alternation is at top level after group closes
Should fail when top-level alternation is disguised by groups on both sides
Should fail when top-level alternation has groups at edges but | is outside them
Should fail when top-level alternation sits between two separate groups
Should fail when null byte is written as hex escape \x00
Should fail when control char is written as hex escape \x01
Should fail when control char is written as hex escape \x0a (newline)
Should fail when control char is written as hex escape \x0d (carriage return)
Should fail when control char is written as hex escape \x1f
Should fail when DEL is written as hex escape \x7f
Should fail when null byte is written as unicode escape \u0000
Should fail when control char is written as unicode escape \u0001
Should fail when control char is written as unicode escape \u001f
Should fail when DEL is written as unicode escape \u007f
Should fail when control char is written as unicode brace escape \u{1}
Should fail when control char is written as unicode brace escape \u{1f}
Should fail when DEL is written as unicode brace escape \u{7f}
Should fail when control character escape \cA is used
Should fail when pattern contains \p{C} (all control/format/surrogate/private-use chars)
Should fail when pattern contains \p{Cc} (control characters, bypasses \0 and \cA checks)
Should fail when pattern contains \p{Cs} (surrogate characters)
Should fail when pattern contains \p{Co} (private use area)
Should fail when pattern contains \p{Control} (long name for \p{Cc})
Should fail when pattern contains \p{Surrogate} (long name for \p{Cs})
Should pass when pattern contains \p{L} (letters, safe allowlist)
Should pass when pattern contains \p{Sc} (currency symbols, safe allowlist)
Should fail when pattern exceeds maxLength (1024)

reject use of non-escaped ., charset ranges should be used instead

tests.description: Should pass when maxLength is set
Should fail when maxLength is not set
Should pass when minLength is a valid safeCount
Should fail when minLength is below 0
Should pass when format and pattern are both set
Should pass when enum and format are both set
Should fail when format is password (annotation-only, provides no content validation)
Should fail when format is binary (annotation-only, provides no content validation)
Should fail when format is just a string (not in allowed list)
Should pass when password field uses pattern for content validation
Should fail when none of const/enum/pattern/format is set
Should pass when contentMediaType is application/json
Should pass when contentMediaType is text/html
Should pass when contentMediaType is a vendor type
Should fail when contentMediaType has invalid top-level type
Should fail when contentMediaType has no subtype
Should pass when contentEncoding is base64
Should pass when contentEncoding is base32
Should pass when contentEncoding is base16
Should pass when contentEncoding is quoted-printable
Should fail when contentEncoding is not in allowed list
Should fail when contentEncoding is unknown
Should fail when pattern contains backreference \1
Should fail when pattern contains backreference \2
Should fail when pattern contains named backreference \k
Should fail when pattern contains null byte escape \0
Should fail when pattern contains \B (non-word boundary)

description: type=string with escaped literal backslash in pattern (false positive regression)

tests.description: Should pass when pattern contains literal backslash before S (\\S)
Should pass when pattern contains literal backslash before W (\\W)
Should pass when pattern contains literal backslash before D (\\D)
Should pass when pattern contains literal backslash before 0 (\\0)
Should pass when pattern contains literal backslash before digit (\\1)
Should pass when pattern contains literal backslash before x00 (\\x00)
Should pass when pattern contains literal backslash before u0000 (\\u0000)
Should pass when pattern contains literal backslash before cA (\\cA)
Should pass when pattern contains literal backslash before k (\\k)
Should fail when triple backslash before 0 (real null byte after literal backslash)
Should pass when quad backslash before 0 (two literal backslashes then zero)

description: type=string with escaped metacharacters in pattern (false positive regression)

tests.description: Should pass when pattern contains escaped literal [ before ^ (\[^...] is literal, not negated class)
Should pass when pattern contains escaped literal ( before ?= (\(?= is literal, not lookahead)
Should pass when pattern contains escaped literal ( before ?<= (\(?<= is literal, not lookbehind)
Should pass when pattern contains escaped literal ) before quantifier (\)+ is literal, not group closer)

description: type=string with bare alternation between groups in pattern

tests.description: Should fail when pattern has bare alternation between groups (breaks anchoring)
Should pass when pattern contains \t (tab literal)
Should pass when pattern contains \b (word boundary)
Should pass when pattern contains \x hex escape
Should pass when pattern contains \u unicode escape
Should pass when pattern contains \d (digit class)
Should pass when pattern contains \w (word class)
Should pass when pattern contains \s (whitespace class)
Should pass when pattern is empty anchors (matches empty string only)

description: type=string with pattern group edge cases

tests.description: Should fail when pattern contains quantified non-capturing group (?:a+)+
Should fail when pattern contains quantified non-capturing group (?:a+)*
Should fail when pattern contains quantified non-capturing group (?:a+){2,}
Should fail when non-capturing group has quantifier (?:abc)+
Should fail when pattern contains named capture group with quantifier (?<name>a+)+
Should pass when pattern contains named capture group without nested quantifier
Should pass when pattern contains \r (literal carriage return, matches CR in input)
Should pass when pattern contains \n (literal newline, matches LF in input)

description: type=string with enum item size limits

tests.description: Should pass when enum items are within size limits
Should fail when enum item string exceeds safe length (4096)
Should pass when contentSchema is used with contentMediaType
Should fail when contentSchema is used without contentMediaType
